Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies PtaCSLA1 as Potri.008G026400 in Methods and Results.

Source_Species.reason: PtaCSLA1 is from poplar; the source material used was poplar 717.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper states poplar 717 is Populus tremula × P. alba.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper explicitly gives poplar 717 as INRA 717-1B4.

Applied_Species.reason: PtaCSLA1 was tested in Arabidopsis csla2,3,9 complementation and in poplar CRISPR knockout analyses.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species are Arabidopsis thaliana and Populus tremula × P. alba, both explicitly stated.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis csla2,3,9 mutant and poplar INRA 717-1B4 are explicitly described.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results support linkage to mannan and glucomannan: mannose content is restored/abolished and PACE indicates glucomannan structure.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Mannan/glucomannan are treated throughout as cell-wall hemicellulosic polysaccharides.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The study is specifically about mannan biosynthesis and secondary cell wall hemicellulose in wood/stems.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results indicate PtaCSLA1 synthesizes secondary cell wall glucomannan backbone in stems/xylem.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: As a CSLA mannan synthase, PtaCSLA1 is directly implicated in polymer backbone synthesis; this is supported by complementation and PACE.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper directly concludes PtaCSLA1 encodes a functional mannan synthase producing mannan/glucomannan.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper supports PtaCSLA1 as a functional CSLA mannan synthase, but the exact formal enzyme name 'Cellulose Synthase-Like A 1 mannan synthase' is not explicitly used.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: PACE data support synthesis of non-patterned glucomannan lacking galactosyl side chains in Arabidopsis; as a mannan synthase it forms the β-(1,4)-linked backbone. Golgi localization, however, is not explicitly shown in the paper.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper does not directly test nucleotide sugar substrates; GDP-mannose/GDP-glucose are plausible but not experimentally shown here.

Primary_Product.reason: Results support production of β-(1,4)-linked glucomannan/mannan polymer via mannose restoration and glucomannan PACE profile.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly states PtaCSLA1 is a CSLA candidate with conserved DDDQxxRW motif and multiple predicted transmembrane domains.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state PtaCSLA1 was the highest expressed putative CSLA in wild-type poplar xylem and the most significantly downregulated in GolS transgenic trees.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Arabidopsis transgene expression under AtCESA7 is explicit, and native function in poplar xylem is supported by knockout effects and xylem-focused expression data.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The constructs used AtCESA7 promoter in pk7WG2, explicitly stated.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Poplar plants were grown in the glasshouse and saccharification was tested with and without acid pretreatment.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Arabidopsis complementation restored mannose, and poplar knockout abolished xylem mannan, supporting a primary role in xylem mannan accumulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are supported by Results: Arabidopsis mannose restoration, non-patterned glucomannan by PACE, mannan abolition in PtaCSLA1 knockout poplar, minimal growth effects, and only marginal saccharification effects.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for PtaCSLA1: Arabidopsis complementation, CRISPR knockout, HPAEC monosaccharide profiling, PACE, and LM21 immunolabeling in poplar.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: While not termed rate-limiting, the paper supports PtaCSLA1 as the major/primary contributor to poplar xylem mannan biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Mannan/glucomannan was assayed in Arabidopsis stems and poplar xylem/secondary cell walls.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames mannan as a structural hemicellulose influencing wall architecture and biomass processing, though effects were modest.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show no substantial growth differences and only marginal saccharification effects in PtaCSLA1 mutants.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states single and double knockouts were indistinguishable in mannan content, implying negligible PtaCSLA2 contribution under tested conditions.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is directly supported by the observed elimination of stem mannan without growth penalty and the authors’ conclusions on engineering mannan content.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly used an RNA-seq expression database from Unda et al. 2017 and does not provide a new accession.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies PgCSLA1 as GCHX01112248.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper specifies the spruce material as Picea engelmannii × Picea glauca × Picea sitchensis.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The spruce clone/source PG29 is explicitly stated.

Applied_Species.reason: PgCSLA1 was tested in Arabidopsis complementation and in poplar overexpression lines.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana and Populus tremula × P. alba are explicitly given.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis csla2,3,9 mutant and poplar INRA 717-1B4 are explicitly described.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results support a link to mannan and glucomannan through mannose quantification, linkage analysis, and PACE.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Mannan/glucomannan are clearly treated as hemicellulosic polysaccharides.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper centers on mannan biosynthesis in secondary cell walls.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Glucomannan backbone synthesis is supported, but 'galactoglucomannan/glucomannan backbone synthesis' is only partly supported because the tested Arabidopsis product lacked galactosyl side chains; spruce native product was not directly characterized in spruce.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PgCSLA1 is shown to function at the polymer backbone synthesis step through complementation and structural analyses.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper directly supports that PgCSLA1 forms mannan/glucomannan product when expressed in plants.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The exact formal name 'Cellulose Synthase-Like A 1 mannan synthase' is not explicitly used, though functional mannan synthase activity is supported.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results support production of non-patterned glucomannan with 4-Man linkages and no detectable galactosyl side chains in Arabidopsis complemented lines.

Primary_Substrate.reason: GDP-mannose and GDP-glucose are not directly tested in this study.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper supports production of β-(1,4)-linked glucomannan/mannan polymer via mannose restoration, linkage analysis, and PACE.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PgCSLA1 is explicitly presented as a CSLA with conserved DDDQxxRW motif and predicted transmembrane domains.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: PgCSLA1 was isolated from spruce xylem tissue, as explicitly described.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RT-qPCR in poplar developing xylem showed transgene expression variation with highest abundance in line 3.

Applied_Promoters.reason: PgCSLA1 was expressed from the AtCESA7 promoter, explicitly stated.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Poplar lines were grown in the glasshouse and saccharification was tested with and without acid pretreatment.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Arabidopsis complementation restored mannan biosynthesis, and poplar overexpression increased stem mannan without growth penalty.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are supported by Results: Arabidopsis complementation, 4-Man linkage recovery, non-patterned glucomannan without galactosyl side chains, up to 11.6% mannan increase in poplar without growth penalty, and slight saccharification improvement strongest in the highest-expressing line.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for PgCSLA1: Arabidopsis complementation, linkage GC-MS, PACE, poplar overexpression, RT-qPCR, and HPAEC.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish rate-limiting status and only supports that PgCSLA1 is sufficient to increase mannan when ectopically expressed.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Mannan/glucomannan was measured in Arabidopsis stems and poplar xylem secondary cell walls.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper treats mannan as a structural wall hemicellulose relevant to wall properties and biomass processing.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results report no significant growth or lignin differences, with only minor sugar changes in some lines.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly discusses PgCSLA1 as a candidate for spruce galactoglucomannan backbone synthesis, yet in Arabidopsis it produced non-galactosylated glucomannan, implying additional factors are needed.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Supported by the demonstrated modest increase in mannan without yield penalty and the authors’ biotechnology discussion.

Omics_Data.reason: RT-qPCR transgene expression data are explicitly reported, and no public accession is given.
